episodes.title: Journalist Anke Richter's Cult Trips
episodes.description:
What happens when a cult journalist finds themselves in a cult? Anke Richter, author of Cult Trip and founder of DECULT, joins Cara to explore the disorienting experience of researching sex cults while simultaneously participating in ISTA, The International School of Temple Arts. Anke describes the dopamine hook of intense retreats that made mundane life feel flat, and the embarrassing realization: "I am one of them."

Anke and Cara examine how organizations systematically dismantle consent through "nature selection" rituals and teachings that reframe physical revulsion as spiritual blockage requiring "work." Anke traces these patterns from Rajneeshpuram to Centerpoint to ISTA, discusses her transition from participant to journalist-activist networker, and shares her current work organizing Australasia's first cult awareness conference through DECULT.

Content Note: Discussion of sexual coercion, child sexual abuse in cults, and consent violations.

Anke Richter is an international journalist based in New Zealand and the author of four non-fiction books. Her last one, Cult Trip: Inside the world of coercion & control (HarperCollins, 2023) is an investigative and personal exploration of former and current sex cults.

She is now the founder and pro bono director of DECULT, a grassroots charity that organised the first cult awareness conference in Australasia and won the Champion of Change Advocate Award from the Freedom Train Project in 2025. Anke received their Ethical Media Award for her work.

https://ankerichter.net/
https://decult.net/

Anke's Social Media:
https://www.facebook.com/ankerichterwriter
https://www.instagram.com/ankerichterwriter/
https://www.linkedin.com/in/anke-richter-writer/

episodes.title: Ellie Wilde's Journey, former ISTA Facilitator
episodes.description:
Former ISTA facilitator Ellie Wilde joins Cara to share her 11-year journey through the organization—from participant to inner circle to speaking out about harm. Ellie arrived at 40, seeking tools for better relationships and sexuality, and found a community where she felt she could finally belong. She traces how she moved from the outer circle into facilitation, exploring what it meant to be part of a world that seemed to offer healing, empowerment, and belonging.

Ellie and Cara discuss themes that emerge in high-control groups: power dynamics between teachers and students, the difference between genuine empowerment and pseudo-importance, and the long work of recovery after leaving. They explore what it takes to find your voice after years of silence, the spiral nature of healing rather than linear progress, and the tension of holding both harm experienced and harm perpetuated. 

CONTENT NOTE
Contains discussion of sexual relationships between teachers and students, power dynamics, and grooming. Brief references to emotional collapse and recovery work.

DISCLAIMER
Views expressed represent individual opinions and experiences, not official positions of the podcast or producers. Content is not intended to malign any religion, group, organization, business, individual, or entity.

ABOUT GUEST
Ellie Wilde is a somatic facilitator, educator, and long-time insider of contemporary tantric and spiritual culture who brings a rare combination of lived experience, depth, and discernment to conversations about power, intimacy, and integrity.

For over a decade, Ellie was a facilitator with the International School of Temple Arts (ISTA), working internationally in immersive trainings focused on sexuality, spirituality, and relational awakening. Her years inside these spaces gave her an intimate understanding of both the profound potential and the serious distortions that can arise when embodiment work, trauma, eros, and spiritual authority are mixed without sufficient maturity, accountability, or nervous-system awareness.

Today, Ellie works primarily with men in one-to-one settings, supporting them to develop emotional literacy, grounded masculinity, and ethical relational capacity. Her work helps men move beyond performance-based spirituality and bypassing, into genuine presence, responsibility, and self-regulation — especially in intimacy, sexuality, and leadership.

In group spaces, Ellie is drawn to facilitating environments that prioritise safety, consent, clarity, and nervous-system coherence. She is particularly passionate about dismantling charismatic power dynamics, reclaiming discernment as a sacred skill, and helping people separate authentic spiritual growth from coercion, fantasy, and unintegrated trauma.

Ellie's voice is informed, compassionate, and unflinching. She speaks not as an outsider throwing stones, but as someone who has loved these worlds, participated deeply in them, and is now committed to telling the truth - so that what is genuinely healing can remain, and what is harmful can finally be named.

www.essentialtantra.org
ellie@essentialtantra.org

RESOURCES
Jane Goodall's Final Message to Humanity
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=your-link-here

Safer Sex-Positive and Spiritual Communities: 3SC.Community

ICSA (International Cultic Studies Association): ICSA.org

CONNECT

episodes.title: My ISTA Story Pt 2 with guest interviewer Jack Rikess
episodes.description:
CONTENT NOTE
Contains discussion of coercion, manipulation, organizational abuse, and victim-blaming. Jack's direct, confrontational interviewing style may be activating for survivors.

In Part 2 (of 2), Cara and Jack explore what comes after you recognize harm in a community you loved. Cara shares her two-year journey attempting accountability through mediation—believing transformation was possible, documenting patterns of harm, and slowly realizing the organization wasn't interested in genuine change. The conversation moves through the stages of disillusionment: from hopeful participant to concerned insider to reluctant whistleblower. Cara reflects on why she stayed in dialogue so long and what it took to finally walk away from the dream of accountability.

Jack and Cara discuss the mechanics of institutional betrayal—how organizations respond to harm reports, the ways victims get blamed for their own trauma, and what happens when mediation becomes performance. Cara describes her current work supporting survivors and building alternative models for community and leadership. 

Part 2 of 2. For Cara's full ISTA origin story, start with Part 1.

DISCLAIMER
Views expressed represent individual opinions and experiences, not official positions of the podcast or producers. Content is not intended to malign any religion, group, organization, business, individual, or entity.

ABOUT JACK RIKESS
Jack Rikess was a stand-up comic for twenty-five years performing across the USA. Along the way he became a producer of comedy shows and other productions, including two years as an Operational Manager for The Emerald Cup. In the early 2000s, he wrote about the Northern California Cannabis scene. That led to a column with Village Voice Media, "Toke of the Town" which was distributed in twenty American cities including LA Weekly, SF Weekly, and City Pages in his hometown of Minneapolis.

His work is featured in Stephen Sparks and Tom Allman's book, "Out There In The Woods"—an account of being embedded with law enforcement in the 36-day manhunt for a double-murderer hiding in the hills of Mendocino County. His first novel, "Just For A Couple Of Weeks" will be published in spring of 2026.

episodes.title: Reading The News: "ISTA in Cyprus: Sacred Sexuality or a Pattern of Harm?"
episodes.description:
Fool's Gold 'Reading The News' Bonus episodes launch with the Politis article, "ISTA in Cyprus: Sacred Sexuality or a Pattern of Harm?" by Katerina Nicolaou, Nov 23, 2025. https://en.politis.com.cy/culture/culture-soul-scene/970088/ista-in-cyprus-sacred-sexuality-or-a-pattern-of-harm

No commentary, just the article as written, read aloud to you by Cara Cordoni.

episodes.title: Commercial Cults with anti-MLM guest Julie Anderson
episodes.description:
What happens when your friend's shampoo post on Facebook leads to five years in a commercial cult? Anti-MLM activist Julie Anderson joins Cara to trace her journey from desperately seeking work to becoming one of the most recognizable voices exposing multi-level marketing fraud. Julie explains how Monat recruited her through product testimonials, bonuses, and "ignorance on fire"—the MLM term for recruits who don't understand what they're actually doing but do it with enthusiasm.

Julie and Cara explore the mechanics of commercial cults: the VIP customer pipeline, and the three-month bonus trap. They discuss how MLMs normalize financial abuse through language control ("it's your business!"), prosperity gospel, and laws literally written by Amway lobbyists. Julie describes her breaking point—a product that caused suicidal ideation, combined with her father's sudden death, and realizing she'd hurt people. 

Content Note: Contains discussion of suicidal ideation, financial exploitation, and manipulation. Brief mention of grief and family loss.

DISCLAIMER:
Views expressed represent individual opinions and experiences, not official positions of the podcast or producers. Content is not intended to malign any religion, group, organization, business, individual, or entity.

ABOUT GUEST
Julie Anderson spent five years in two commercial cults simultaneously—the multi-level marketing company Monat and the MLM coaching group Rank Makers. After realizing the full scope of what she'd been part of, she turned everything she'd learned about content creation and recruitment into a weapon against the industry itself. For four years, Julie became one of the most recognizable anti-MLM voices online, exposing the mechanics of commercial fraud and helping countless people recognize and exit exploitative schemes.

This is Julie's final interview. After nine years of creating content maniacally—five years in, four years speaking out—she has stepped away from social media to rest and heal. Her extensive body of work remains available to help others in the years to come.
YouTube: https://youtube.com/@JulieAndersonvideos

RESOURCES:
Safer Sex-Positive and Spiritual Communities: 3SC.Community
ICSA (International Cultic Studies Association): ICSA.org

ABOUT
Fool's Gold: Discernment in the Age of Grift explores spiritual seeking, coercive control, and how we develop discernment. Hosted by Cara Cordoni—leadership coach, cooperative developer, and cultic harm survivor—the podcast examines what happens when the search for meaning collides with manipulation.

"I started Fool's Gold as a shared inquiry space—a place to wrestle with hard questions about spiritual seeking, consent, and how people end up in harmful groups. I'm working through my own experiences with the International School of Temple Arts (ISTA), a neo-tantra organization that turned out to be a cult. Not my first cult, either.

Why Fool's Gold? I am the fool. We are all fools. Most of us have been fooled. How do we learn to sort the gold from the glitter?

Some questions we explore: What is consent? What is coercion? What makes us vulnerable to undue influence? How do we maintain the balance between bringing our full selves to our endeavors and disappearing into them?

These questions matter beyond cults. People don't join cults—they join communities, trainings, movements that promise transformation or belonging or purpose. Losing ourselves can happen anywhere: relationships, workplaces, spiritual communities, political movements.

I don't have all the answers. I'm still figuring out what happened to me. But I have questions, and I've invited thoughtful people—survivors, journalists, researchers, activists, legal experts—to explore them with us.

Drawing on my experience attempting to hold ISTA accountable through the activist group 3SC (Safer Sex-Positive and Spiritual Communities), I bring both lived experience and endless questions about power, accountability, and repair.

This is a space for thinking through what we couldn't see at the time and building the discernment we wish we'd had."

CREDITS:
Production Team: Paxton Scarberry, Shanti Shakavarti, Jon Friedman
Opening Graphics: Paxton Scarberry
Theme Music: "Midnight Rendezvous" by Sasha Ende (ende.app)
Filmed at: Access Humboldt
Licensed under Creative Commons BY-NC 4.0

episodes.title: My ISTA Story, Part 1 - with interviewer Jack Rikess
episodes.description:
What happens when a journalist flips the script? In this episode, writer and comedian Jack Rikess interviews host Cara Cordoni about her journey into and out of The International School of Temple Arts (ISTA). Cara walks Jack through her three ISTA retreats, from the $2,500 promise of transformation to the reality of large group awareness training (LGAT) manipulation—including sleep disruption, psychological pressure, and the dopaminergic highs that feel like enlightenment but can also be induced mania.

Jack brings his journalist's instinct for direct, no-holds-barred questioning ("And what exactly happens with that carrot ritual? "). His style is confrontational but not hostile—he challenges, asks uncomfortable questions about sexuality and polyamory, and isn't afraid to express his opinion. Together, they find both absurdity and darkness in Cara's story, exploring how bright people miss obvious red flags and why she initially believed ISTA could be reformed.

TRIGGER WARNING: Contains explicit discussion of sexual practices, manipulation, and coercive group dynamics. Jack's direct, challenging interviewing style may be activating for some survivors.

DISCLAIMER:
Views expressed represent individual opinions and experiences, not official positions of the podcast or producers. Content is not intended to malign any religion, group, organization, business, individual, or entity.

ABOUT GUEST
Jack Rikess was a stand-up comic for twenty-five years performing across the USA. Along the way he became a producer of comedy shows and other productions, including two years as an Operational Manager for The Emerald Cup. In the early 2000s, he wrote about the Northern California Cannabis scene. That led to a column with Village Voice Media, "Toke of the Town" which was distributed in twenty American cities including LA Weekly, SF Weekly, and City Pages in his hometown of Minneapolis.

His work is featured in Stephen Sparks and Tom Allman's book, "Out There In The Woods"—an account of being embedded with law enforcement in the 36-day manhunt for a double-murderer hiding in the hills of Mendocino County.
His first novel, "Just For A Couple Of Weeks" will be published in spring of 2026.
